name: Polymorphism & Phase Transformation
description: Polymorphic systems can crystallize into several structures: kinetics often favor a metastable form first (Ostwald's rule of stages) while thermodynamics favor the most stable polymorph, and applying temperature, pressure, or a seed crosses the transformation barrier to convert toward the controlled target polymorph.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Multiple polymorphs exist"]
    N2["Rank stable vs metastable"]
    N3{"Kinetic or thermodynamic?"}
    N4["Metastable forms first"]
    N5["Ostwald's rule of stages"]
    N6["Most stable nucleates"]
    N7["Apply T / P / seed"]
    N8{"Barrier crossed?"}
    N9["Anneal / add seed"]
    N10["Converted polymorph"]
    N11["Controlled polymorph"]

    N1 --> N2

    N8 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11
